mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding household income and aggregate demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Payrolls expanded to 146659K in Sep 2017 continuing a multi-year uptrend
failure_condition: Over any 2-year rolling window, the directional accuracy of PAYEMS trend on 20-day SPX returns falls below 52%, or the mean forward return following positive PAYEMS momentum is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the federal funds rate of at least 25 basis points over a 3-month window signals a restrictive monetary shift that raises discount rates and debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate at 1.15% in Sep 2017 amid hiking cycle
failure_condition: In triggered periods, the proportion of negative 20-day SPX returns is less than 55%, or the average forward return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad-based demand expansion that is not driven by credit or temporary factors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Sep 2017 retail sales at 483895M and payrolls at 146659K both rising
failure_condition: The joint signal from RSAFS and PAYEMS produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the 60-day forward return is not higher when both series rise together than when only one rises.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, an increase in the unemployment rate of more than 20 basis points over 6 months signals deteriorating labor market momentum that precedes earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% in Sep 2017 near cycle lows
failure_condition: Triggered episodes in late-cycle regimes produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the strategy Sharpe ratio over any 5-year window is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates contracting manufacturing output and weaker capacity utilization, which reduces corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when INDPRO declines, or the mean 5-day return following a decline is not negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative drop in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 5 percent over 3 months signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of episodes, or the rank correlation between HOUST momentum and 60-day SPX returns is positive over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms above-trend economic momentum that supports earnings surprises, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered quarters produce negative 60-day forward returns more than 45% of the time, or the mean 60-day return following high GDPC1 growth is not statistically greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) combined with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) compress real interest rates and inflate nominal corporate earnings while reducing debt-service burdens in real terms, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 246.373 and FEDFUNDS at 1.15% in Sep 2017
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 5-day returns occur in less than 55% of jointly signaled periods.
